rag_rules: Repair only by aligning AGREE clauses with visible AADL features or declared eq helpers; never invent declarations.
Move implementation-level bindings to implementation annexes; never place guarantees in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Preserve type consistency: Boolean expressions must use bool-typed variables, numeric expressions must use int/real.
Use only identifiers explicitly visible in the current component scope—no subcomponent paths or package references unless declared.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'assign' only for output features or auxiliary variables in implementation annexes.
Guarantees and assumptions must use AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not resolve unresolved identifiers by adding new declarations—delete invalid clauses instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
